rationale: The two papers share a central construct: the citing study’s main outcome is increased no-buy/choice deferral, while the cited study is specifically about how buy/no-buy response mode changes deferral and evaluation process. That makes the cited work a plausible theory/mechanism foundation for the citing paper’s deferral arguments, so this is not safe to treat as an obvious incidental mention (even though the raw evidence here is just a single DOI-matched reference).
